math_recheck: Re-derived all load-bearing eqs against Kramers 1940 (visual pages 9-16): FPE(9), parabolic-barrier ansatz/condition (2pi w')^2=a(a-eta) and upper-sign selection a-eta>0 (21), flux w=KT sqrt(2pi eta T/a) via integration-by-parts (24), n_A=K(T/w)sqrt(2pi eta T/(a-eta)), transmission factor kappa=sqrt(1+(eta/2 w_b)^2)-eta/2 w_b with both limits, high-friction 2pi w w'/eta (17), low-friction eta Q/T (28), TST one-way flux=T; all confirmed symbolically (sympy) and numerically. Both deliverable code blocks run and asserts pass.
fixes: reasoning.md:138 + answer.md:75 + notes/synthesis.md:40 — corrected the TST-validity band UPPER bound from 'a few x 2pi w'' (where kappa~0.3, a 70% error) to Kramers' own ~1.2 w' (ordinary freq, ~w_b/5, kappa~0.9); verified kappa(eta=1.2 w')=0.91 vs kappa(eta=2pi w')=0.62

reasoning.md:169 + answer.md (demo) + code/kramers_rate.py:56 — fixed factor-of-2pi error in barrier action Ib: was Eb/omega0 (angular) giving I_c=1.59, correct action I(E)=2*pi*E/omega0=E/f giving I_c=10; numerically confirmed harmonic action; turnover peak now at eta/w_b~0.016 (was wrongly 0.097), k_peak/k_TST~0.99 (was 0.95), matching the prose crossing condition eta~w*T/E_b
answer.md:133-136 — updated reported output (peak eta/w_b 0.1->0.016, k_peak/k_TST 0.95->0.99) to match corrected code
